item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article could not be sourced. Thus, it is assumed that the image is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and has been removed.

item.title: Interesting
item.content:encoded: This page is the lowest numbered SCP with a lower score than its number. As of 12:43 CST on 10/01/19, it has a score of +76.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[DATA EXPUNGED]itored

What possible reason could there be to censor the first part of the word 'monitored'? In fact, it seems quite a lot of information was censored that had no need to be censored.

That aside, the idea is rather cool, and the Addendum adds a creep factor entirely separate from the SCP itself. So no vote.

item.title: Re: a little error
item.content:encoded: It's correct. The author (pooryoric) has a particular style of expungment.

item.title: a little error
item.content:encoded:
"the [DATA EXPUNGED]itored constantly for signs of psychological aberration."

is this correct and I am reading it wrong?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this post initially. It reads well and generally is definitively creepy… Until you start reading about the second house. At which point its like being hit by a [Data Expunged] sledgehammer. It seems like this was put in place to add mystery, but really just creates confusion. Reflush out the second house, minimizing what is blanked out, that or just expunge it entirely, stating only that there is a second anomalous house. I THINK Shayat has the right idea of the house, though this does not explain the state of decomposition described, which also doesn't explain what exactly is found to BE decomposed.

Fixing the missing data on the second house will remove my -1 and turn it into a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see that Yoric hasn't been here since Mid-2014, but this is in desperate need of some polishing. It's showing its age rather badly, and the "Yoricspungement" obscures too much for me to have any idea of what goes on in -2.

-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one feels honestly undercooked for me. I know it's old-ish, but it still feels like I'm seriously missing a bunch of the interesting stuff here. No, I'm talking about the expungement, I'm talking about the overall implied universe. I don't know, it just bugs me in a way other shorter skips don't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know about anyone else, but I'm dying to see the interview logs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The creep factor of what a willfully intangible man could do is what got me
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The creepy sex thing is as decent hook in and of itself, but the existence of 102-02 is hampering the overall impact. 01 is, as Shayat said, a sex offender's dream come true, but that hook gets superseded by 02. However, when it comes time to rehook the reader and convince them to be creeped out by the second house, you get slapped upside the head with redactions and expungements. All the impact is lost, and the article falls flat right in front of the finish line. No vote for me.

item.title: Changed my vote
item.content:encoded:
I had downvoted this article a while ago, mostly because I don't really care for MC&D as a hook, but re-reading this right now made me change my mind.

I like how it's a sex offender's vacation dream house, but I would really like to see some logs shedding a little (just a wee bit) of light on the workings of SCP-102-2. I like vague SCPs that make you finish the story in your own mind, but I'm drawing a complete blank about the second house.

The only thing I can think of is, that since the first one makes you incorporeal so you can go outside and easily rape people, the second one, somehow makes it easier for the occupant to be raped. That would explain the psychological trauma and the need for steroids to enhance the damaged musculature from being held still for an extended amount of time. Maybe ghostly figures hold you and mollest you for the duration of the lease, or something like that.

I know it's been a while, but am I close to what you intended here, Yoric?

Anyway, it's very creepy in how it's an anomalous object tailored to fulfill horrible sexual fantasies, regardless of MC&D. So I upvoted it this time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I thought the same about the "monitored" thing… And the data expunged stuff seems to be used a bit much. I kinda like it and kinda don't, so no vote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is a writing trick/style specific to the author pooryoric. If I recall the idea is a rushed or lazy censor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: why would the expunging end half way through the word "monitored" I take issue with a lot of the lazy writing done with the [data expunged] and [redacted] but that is just stupid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Might be a problem with my computer, but I'm not seeing the image, just the image file name.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Obligatory this-is-featured post. I like this one, but never loved it, it just seems to be missing something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've done a few minor wording edits to clarify the effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I keep forgetting to write them…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Logs would be awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd say write them, concentrating on 102-2 and the nice [DATA EXPUNGED] it seems to afflict people with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pleasantly surprised people like this article. I was honestly pretty on the fence about posting it. My question now is should I write those interview logs, or is that unnecissary? I'm trending toward not writing them, because I garauntee I can't come up with anything as good as what you guys do in your heads, but on the other hand, I do like when SCP articles have a bit of fluff attached.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This made me go from "I want one" to "Ah" to "Oh god" in a suitable amount of time for me to upvote this article. I like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad someone got that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The second hut creeps me out. The first one is just a Make-A-Kitty-Pryde-But-With-Invisiblity-Too generator…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh good lord, the addendum. Augh. MC&D creepiness cranked up to eleven. O_o

+1.

item.title: spelling.
item.content:encoded:
your spelling, it was terrible.

but i still like it
